differentialBuckets.action: Early risk testing if high risk.

differentialBuckets.label: Key thresholds and interpretation caveats: One-step 75-g OGTT for GDM: fasting >=92, 1-hour >=180, or 2-hour >=153 mg/dL. Two-step approach if used: 50-g screen often >=130-140 mg/dL; 100-g Carpenter-Coustan thresholds fasting 95, 1-hour 180, 2-hour 155, 3-hour 140 mg/dL, usually 2 abnormal values.

initialTests.label: Early risk testing if high risk.

source.source_section: Gestational Diabetes generated endocrine workup: diagnostic workup
source.implementation_notes: Generated from guideline-backed endocrine workup automation; schema, source, PHI, and regression tests run on 2026-06-06.

initialTests.action: Interpret with local lab ranges. Reference anchors: One-step 75-g OGTT for GDM: fasting >=92, 1-hour >=180, or 2-hour >=153 mg/dL. Two-step approach if used: 50-g screen often >=130-140 mg/dL; 100-g Carpenter-Coustan thresholds fasting 95, 1-hour 180, 2-hour 155, 3-hour 140 mg/dL, usually 2 abnormal values. A1c: normal <5.7%; prediabetes 5.7-6.4%; diabetes >=6.5%. Fasting plasma glucose: normal <100 mg/dL; prediabetes 100-125; diabetes >=126. 2-hour 75-g OGTT: normal <140 mg/dL; prediabetes 140-199; diabetes >=200. Random plasma glucose >=200 mg/dL with classic symptoms supports diabetes diagnosis. Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects.

initialTests.label: 24-28 week one-step 75-g OGTT or local two-step strategy.

reference_values: One-step 75-g OGTT for GDM: fasting >=92, 1-hour >=180, or 2-hour >=153 mg/dL.
Two-step approach if used: 50-g screen often >=130-140 mg/dL; 100-g Carpenter-Coustan thresholds fasting 95, 1-hour 180, 2-hour 155, 3-hour 140 mg/dL, usually 2 abnormal values.
Fasting plasma glucose: normal <100 mg/dL; prediabetes 100-125; diabetes >=126.
Random plasma glucose >=200 mg/dL with classic symptoms supports diabetes diagnosis.
Use local laboratory intervals and assay-specific cutoffs; endocrine immunoassays are affected by pregnancy, age, sex, acute illness, binding proteins, biotin, and medication effects.
